SHUBHANG SHARMA.
$\sum_i\varphi_i$
~ MAY 03.2026, HYDERABAD.

ACADEMIC
CREATIVE
MATH-PH
FIELDNOTES
BLOG

Pushing Symbols

Yes, math is mostly about pushing symbols and reaching answers, questions and all of that. But it is mostly natural too! There is so much going on behind those symbol pushing. We can almost feel it, the process of math becomes then to rigorously put everything down. So yes, symbol pushing, sometimes unpredictably perhaps and sometimes predictably, but there seems to be a context almost always. And that’s why it’s a science. Because there is meaning behind those results and proofs - behind those symbol pushings.


May 4, 2026

Without audacity nothing begins. Without delusion nothing gets finished.


May 3, 2026

A break

Last weekend, I was already on a break from studying. I needed it. After trying to work a couple of days after that, I once again find myself pausing it. In the last two days, I took a break. I took a break to energise myself with some academic motivation. It’s very linear sometimes, just reading reading, checking off section by section, chapter by chapter, without any sense of a medium-term goal. There is a short term goal, and then there’s a long-term goal that I can tick off. Now that I know it’s going to take a lot of time into the Linear Algebra book, I feel a little unready to face the time.


May 1, 2026

The gentle nights

It’s probably more than a month now, since I started sleeping late into the night, early into the next day… It’s so quiet, with no background pertubations. It’s just me with my works.


Apr 29, 2026

A tangential hope


8 minutes past twelve today, I received an update from the mentorship team at SGB. Rejection :). The journey around this application was a growing experience! Not gonna hide, it’s sad, and I have been spiraling down ever since. But let me try to pause that and note a few things I am proud of first :).

Apr 13, 2026

The Posterior Chain & a complete home strength workout plan

I had my free Root Cause Analysis session today in the morning. A doctor from Granimals team joined online, asked about my history and location of the pain. Then she checked my movement patterns and pains.

April, here we go

Ah, I am very embarrassed by my efforts to read basic math all these months. Of course, I have talked about it time and again throughout the last year. But it must do it, finish it.

Nov 18, 2025

Consistency and perturbations

Sticking to the februarry master’s deadline. Want to get to my pre-surgery consistency. Started working in a more organized way today - basically alternating study sessions and a 10 minute walk reset.

Career
Nov 3, 2025

The non-triviality of my academic efforts ft. Septoplasty

The surgery pushed me to the rock bottom in my physical capacity to bear things and do things! I never had such a painful event before and such a daunting recovery curve. It’s been 2 weeks since the surgery now, and I have had many thoughts in that time.

Career
Oct 24, 2025

In making.

Almost every thing I want to do is in making, or rather in learning. I am learning to fold, learning to code js for web, learning algebra, analysis, topology, learning to understand social structures better, and what not. This is an interesting stage I am in. I am building myself. And it will take at least an year to reap some fruit out of this and get a place in this world! But as usual, imma try enjoying this process. Respecting this phase, also like a stranger, and standing still when I feel lost – for, the forest knows where I am…


Jul 21, 2025

The first IRCTC parcel experience

The parcel service provided by IRCTC is too good to be true. It’s just the ground level staff that messes up the whole game – probably just like every other case of a good government scheme. I just graduated (yay!) from NISER and had to move some stuff away from Bhubaneswar to home – my 29T cycle, lots of books on pscyhology, math and physics, and Indian Philosophy. The following is a report of the whole story.

Experiences
Jul 15, 2025

Plumbing hat

Occasionally in one of the bathrooms of my parents’ house, there is a stinky sewage odor. My sister has been trying ways to get rid of it, and even called a plumber last week. However, the timing of the plumber was out of the odor one, and he indicated no problem with the drain hole. He asked about the vent – it’s channeled to the terrace of the apartment and our flat is on the first floor, so ruled out. He also noted that there are water in the drain hole and that means, no smell/gas can enter.
